flashcards.front: What is point processing in digital image processing?
flashcards.back: Point processing transforms pixel values based solely on their own intensity, without considering neighboring pixels. The output at coordinate (x,y) depends only on the input value at (x,y).

flashcards.front: Define image negative and its formula.
flashcards.back: Image negative is obtained by inverting pixel values. Formula: s = L - 1 - r, where r is input intensity, s is output, L is number of gray levels (typically 256).

flashcards.front: What is log transformation in point processing?
flashcards.back: Log transformation compresses the dynamic range of images with large variations. Formula: s = c × log(1 + r), where c is a constant. It expands dark regions while compressing bright regions.
Power-law transformation: s = c × r^γ. When γ < 1, it expands dark regions; when γ > 1, it expands bright regions. Used for gamma correction in displays.
Contrast stretching is a piecewise linear transformation that expands the range of gray levels in an image. It enhances the contrast by stretching the intensity range to fill the full display range.
Bit-plane slicing separates an image into binary images representing each bit position (0-7 for 8-bit images). Higher bit planes contain more significant visual information.
Intensity level slicing highlights specific gray-level ranges. It sets pixels in the desired range to a bright value and all others to dark (or vice versa), enabling isolation of features.
Histogram equalization redistributes gray levels to uniformly distribute the histogram. It enhances contrast by mapping input gray levels to output levels using cumulative distribution function.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between point processing and neighborhood processing?
flashcards.back: Point processing: output depends only on input pixel value at same location. Neighborhood processing: output depends on pixel and its neighbors (used in filtering, convolution).

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the transformation function s = T(r)?
flashcards.back: The transformation function T(r) maps input intensity r to output intensity s. It is the fundamental mathematical representation of any point processing operation.

flashcards.front: Define dynamic range in image processing.
flashcards.back: Dynamic range refers to the ratio between maximum and minimum pixel intensities an image can contain. Point processing can compress or expand the dynamic range.
Piecewise linear transformation uses different linear formulas for different intensity ranges. It allows custom contrast enhancement in specific regions while compressing others.
Histogram specification (matching) transforms an image to have a specified histogram shape, unlike equalization which produces uniform histogram. It provides controlled contrast enhancement.

flashcards.front: Why is log transformation also called compression transformation?
flashcards.back: Log transformation compresses high intensity values while expanding low values. Since the log function grows slowly, large input variations produce smaller output variations.

flashcards.front: What is the value of c in log transformation formula?
flashcards.back: Constant c = 255 / log(1 + L-1), where L is maximum gray level (typically 256). This normalizes output to full display range [0, 255].
